meta.title: W246 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes W246 engine — M270, OM651, OM607. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the OM651 chain risk explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes W246 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned W246 engine typically costs £1,200–£5,500 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], depending on engine — the OM607 1.5 diesel is the cheapest at £1,200–£2,800, while the OM651 2.1 diesel runs £3,200–£5,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,100. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes W246 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M270 1.6‑litre turbo petrol (B180/B200) is the most dependable choice — it has no timing‑chain weakness and its cooling‑system issues are well‑understood and relatively inexpensive to fix. The OM607 1.5 diesel (Renault‑sourced) also proves robust if serviced properly. The OM651 diesel needs careful chain‑history checking.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes W246?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651 timing‑chain tensioner is the single biggest risk — cold‑start rattle is the warning sign. M270 petrol engines suffer from water‑pump and coolant‑pipe leaks, while OM607 (1.5 diesel) units often develop injector wear. All W246 models can experience DPF clogging on diesel variants used mainly for short trips.

It depends heavily on the engine. Petrol M270 units are generally reliable with routine maintenance. The OM651 diesel is robust but carries a timing‑chain tensioner risk that requires proactive inspection. The OM607 (Renault K9K) is simpler and cheaper to maintain but injector wear is a known pattern. Overall, a well‑maintained W246 can be a dependable family car.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes W246 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright “avoid” — but a pre‑2015 OM651 without documented chain‑tensioner replacement is the one we consistently advise against. A neglected M270 petrol with persistent coolant leaks also warrants caution. Always insist on full service history and a pre‑purchase inspection by a specialist.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my W246 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs include: cold‑start rattle (OM651 chain), persistent coolant loss (M270 water pump), rough idle or hesitation (OM607 injectors), or blue smoke from the exhaust. If you experience any of these, have the car diagnosed immediately — catching a problem early saves thousands.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes W246 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £8,000 W246, a £3,200–£5,500 OM651 recon is 40–70% of the car's value — safe if the car is otherwise sound. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon is 80–140% — walk away. Our 70% Mercedes threshold is the line.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes W246 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper servicing, the M270 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles, and the OM607 diesel is known to reach 200,000+ miles in Renault applications. The OM651's life is limited by the timing‑chain tensioner — addressed proactively, it can comfortably manage 150,000+ miles.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the W246, W245 and W247?

acceptedAnswer.text: The W245 is the first‑generation B‑Class (2005–2011) with a taller, more MPV‑like body. The W246 (2012–2018) is the second generation on the MFA platform, sharing underpinnings with the A‑Class and GLA. The W247 (2019–present) is the third generation, built on the MFA2 platform with updated engines and tech.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes W246 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and in the vehicle documentation. The code is also stamped on the engine block — on OM651 units it's on the front left, on M270 it's on the right side. Tell us your variant and registration year, and we'll identify it for you.

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes B‑Class W246 Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: OM651 ratings: 95% confidence, inherited from the Model Page where one exists. M270 and OM607: Expert‑knowledge tier, derived from cross‑referencing specialist and owner sources — confirm against the Model Page before publishing.

overview.intro: The W246 is the second generation of the Mercedes‑Benz B‑Class, a five‑door hatchback produced from late 2011 to late 2018. Built on the Mercedes‑Benz MFA platform, it shares its underpinnings with the W176 A‑Class, X156 GLA, and C117 CLA. The W246 was offered with a choice of petrol, diesel, and natural‑gas engines, plus a battery‑electric variant (W242). It is classified as a small MPV by Euro NCAP.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz MFA • Predecessor: W245 • Successor: W247 • Sibling body styles in this generation: none (W246 is a single 5‑door hatchback body style).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No W246‑specific enquiry figure is available; OM651 diesel enquiries dominate the broader Mercedes compact‑car parc, with 1,250 UK requests in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.ownershipVerdict: Strong Performer — more power, same upkeep
Budget Diesel — cheap to run, cheap to fix
Watch the Chain — tensioner is the key risk
Best Diesel — torque and economy, if chain is sound

economicsRule.title: The W246 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£4,800 OM651 recon against a £6,000 W246 sits at 47–80% of the car’s value. On a sub‑£5,000 example, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we’ll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well‑kept £8,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 35–60% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W246 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM607 1.5 diesel — cheapest recon and simple repairs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W246 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM651 2.1 diesel — timing‑chain tensioner work is labour‑intensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W246 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: M270 1.6 petrol — reliable, no chain risk, and cheaper than the 2.0‑litre.

problems.title: OM651 Timing‑Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.affectedModels: W246 B180 CDI, B200 CDI, B220 CDI (all OM651 variants).
problems.rootCause: The timing‑chain tensioner can wear prematurely, leading to chain slack and, in severe cases, catastrophic engine failure. Cold‑start rattle is the classic early warning.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, get the chain tensioner inspected immediately — a £600–£1,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £2,800–£5,500 replacement that follows if ignored. On a W246 worth under £5,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare OM651 engine prices for your W246 →

problems.title: M270 Water Pump & Coolant Leaks
problems.affectedModels: W246 B180, B200, B220 4MATIC, B250 (all M270 petrol variants).
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump housing and coolant crossover pipes can crack with age and heat cycling, leading to gradual coolant loss and potential overheating.
problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £300–£600 water pump replacement is cheap insurance against a £1,500+ head‑gasket repair if the engine overheats.

cta.label: Compare M270 engine prices for your W246 →

problems.title: OM607 Injector Wear (Renault K9K)
problems.affectedModels: W246 B160 CDI, B180 CDI (OM607 1.5 diesel).
problems.rootCause: Injector wear and electrical faults are common on the Renault‑sourced K9K unit, leading to rough idle, poor economy, and increased emissions.
problems.recommendation: If your OM607‑powered W246 develops a rough idle or hesitation, have the injectors tested immediately — a £200–£500 repair is far cheaper than the £1,200+ replacement that follows a full failure.

cta.label: Compare OM607 engine prices for your W246 →

replacementCosts.h2: W246 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

OM651 costs carried forward from the EM data’s Table A.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are W246 5‑door hatchback only. The W242 Electric Drive is a separate model and is not covered on this page.
